content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 18, 2002

Colin J. Coffey, General Counsel
Archer • Norris
Post Office Box 8035
Walnut Creek, CA 94596-3728

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-264

Dear Mr. Coffey:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Donald Newman, M.D. regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); 18329(a) (copy enclosed).) However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice where the requestor is seeking advice that requires us to evaluate conduct that has already occurred. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) In addition, the Commission may also decline to provide written advice if it would be "inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest" to do so. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(F).)

Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. In addition, your current question involves analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred, such that issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111.

Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your request. In addition, nothing in the Act prevents the official from seeking legal advice from you or private counsel.

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-02-264
Page No. 2

Should you have any further questions, please contact the Commission at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: [signature]
Galena West
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure

GW:jg
E:\AdviceLtrs\W-02-264

sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a declination letter with clear reasoning provided. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers, but substantive explanation of declination grounds is present. Requestor identity and subject matter are identifiable.
